Consider a strictly increasing differentiable function f whose domain is real numbers, i.e. for any
x, y ∈ R with x < y then f(x) < f(y). Consider any three numbers a, b, c, such that a < c < b and
a ≠ b ≠ c, following are two statements for this consideration:
S1 : There can be the situation where f ′(c) < 0.
S2 : f ′(b) > f ′(a) satisfied always.
Which of the above statements are correct?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
